steps.title: Find the key phrase
steps.content: The passage says "if one has a ______ that the word 'own' has a high incidence" and that "an analysis of a corpus can support that hypothesis." The blank is an unproven belief that can later be confirmed by data.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean an unverified assumption or guess. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — synopsis: "if one has a synopsis" — synopsis means a brief summary. You don't test a summary with a corpus. ✗
  • Choice B — scheme: "if one has a scheme" — scheme means a plan or plot. A scheme is not something a corpus can support or refute. ✗
  • Choice C — recognition: "if one has a recognition" — recognition means acknowledgment of something already known. The passage is about testing something not yet confirmed. ✗
  • Choice D — supposition: "if one has a supposition" — supposition means an assumption made without proof. This matches perfectly: the corpus then tests whether the supposition holds. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
